name: Wa'ahila Ridge Trail
tagline: A proper ridge hike that starts right behind the University of Hawaii — lunch break adventure material.
parking: Free parking at Wa'ahila Ridge State Recreation Area off Ruth Place — small lot, comes with restrooms and picnic tables
bestTime: Late afternoon for golden hour light on the ridge — but start early enough to finish before gate closes
talkStory: The ironwood and Cook pine forest at the trailhead was planted by the CCC in the 1930s, transforming bare hillsides into the lush canopy you walk through today. The ridge separates Manoa and Palolo valleys, which ancient Hawaiians cultivated extensively for taro. UH students have used this as a study-break escape for decades since the trailhead is minutes from campus. The narrow upper ridge delivers views from Diamond Head to the Ko'olau summit.
